updated_conclusion: The mapped login/settings provider descriptors now have native consumer xref evidence. pe-address-refs finds two RIP-relative LEA references for each of the five known descriptor rows: one non-clone setup site that installs or passes the descriptor, and one clone/materializer thunk that stamps the descriptor into copied provider objects.
evidence_source: Raw broad scan sample with refs: win64c-provider-descriptor-scan-rdata-top40-with-refs.json.

native_recognizers: aligned non-executable-section qwords equal to target VA/RVA
common x64 RIP-relative memory operands such as LEA/MOV/CMP/TEST forms
per-target hit counts with capped returned hit lists
tooling_milestone.provider_scan_integration: pe-provider-descriptor-scan --include-refs attaches pe-address-refs result rows to each returned descriptor.

new_observations: The setup reference versus clone-materializer reference split is consistent across all five already-mapped login/settings descriptors.
The Config descriptor setup reference 0x1403D8DE2 matches the previously mapped 0x1403D8E7A callsite setup window, giving the native scanner a cross-check against manual Ghidra evidence.
The broad top-40 provider scan sample also shows two references per descriptor in the first observed family, which suggests the setup-plus-clone pattern generalizes beyond login/settings rows.
No aligned non-executable qword references were returned for the five known rows in the capped hit lists; the useful consumer evidence is code-side RIP-relative LEA materialization.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.provider-descriptor-scan-rdata: Adds consumer references to the discovered descriptor population so rows can be grouped by setup sites instead of only .rdata order.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-provider-descriptor-nativ: Separates each descriptor's clone thunk reference from its setup/consumer reference.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-settings-config-provider-and-rtti-: Confirms the Config setup site through native reference scanning at 0x1403D8DE2.

bounded_inference.statement: For these provider adapter descriptors, the non-clone RIP-relative LEA is the highest-value next waypoint: it identifies where the client constructs, publishes, or passes the provider row into a surrounding subsystem, while the clone-materializer LEA is table mechanics.
bounded_inference.confidence: high for reference locations and clone/setup split, medium for final semantic labels until each setup window is traced

next_targets: Run focused callsite-argument and instruction-window passes at setup references 0x140024D4E, 0x14018E0EF, 0x1400CCD53, and 0x1400CCE40.
Use the --include-refs provider scan output to cluster the 378 candidates by setup-site function before assigning subsystem labels.
Add compact function/window grouping to pe-address-refs so consumer evidence can be summarized without separate Ghidra passes.
Continue feeding these setup references into bootstrap, JS5, login, and settings object-lifetime maps.
